inputsInspected.notes: Confirmed source-row-first intent, compact-control removal, exporter scope, and success criteria.
Confirmed canonical sourceRowValidation.rows requirements and compact-control removal scenarios.
Confirmed 155-row catalog inventory, source-row grouping, and safe evidence requirements.
Confirmed verify/archive consume canonical JSON summaries and must not duplicate matrices.
Confirmed exporter default table path and pytest expectations.
Confirmed security-impacting classification, safe evidence policy, artifact authority, and exporter risk boundaries.
Confirmed TD/NA/MD/PY cases and unavailable-tooling carry-forward expectations.
All planned apply tasks 1.1 through 4.4 are checked complete.
Cumulative slice evidence records source-row contract, downstream propagation, and Python exporter test results.
Changed-file context inspected through launch status, git status, and apply evidence; no implementation files were modified during review.

testRunner.evidence: Executed during review: 20 passed in 0.90s on Python 3.12.6 / pytest 9.1.1.

build.evidence: openspec/config.yaml and apply-progress.md report no global build runner for this repository.

coverage.evidence: No global coverage runner detected; not claimed as passing evidence.

linter.evidence: No global linter detected; git diff --check passed with line-ending normalization warnings only.

typeChecker.evidence: No global type checker detected; not claimed as passing evidence.

formatter.evidence: No global formatter detected; not claimed as passing evidence.

runtimeChecks.unavailableToolingNote: Repository-wide build, coverage, lint, type-check, and format commands are unavailable; Python exporter tests are available and passed. Unavailable tooling is carried as warning evidence only.

evidenceSummary.summary: Apply evidence is coherent with proposal/spec/design/test-design/tasks. All apply tasks are complete, Python exporter tests passed, and source-row-first contract evidence is ready for dedicated security review.
items: Apply evidence records three bounded slices: security-review contract, downstream/shared/agent propagation, and Python exporter/tests/README.
Review confirmed active security-review skill/reference files no longer contain compactControlValidation, SEC-* authority markers, compactGuidelines, compactMapping, or Compact Control sections.
Review confirmed shared, verify, archive, and agent wrappers consume canonical sourceRowValidation.rows summaries/refs without copying or re-scoring the matrix.
python -m pytest python/tests passed with 20 tests, including sourceRowValidation.rows default, compact-only default failure, workbook read-back, safe CLI errors, and README assertions.

operationalEvidenceSummary.summary: Operational impact is limited to repository artifacts, generated reports, and optional workbook exports. No production runtime, hostnames, logs, payloads, credentials, or workbook bytes are required or recorded.
items: Safe evidence uses paths, section anchors, sanitized summaries, command outcomes, and unavailable-tooling statements only.
No generated .xlsx files were found in the repository; exporter tests use temporary output paths.
README warns that the exporter does not sanitize unsafe source JSON and generated workbooks should not be committed as ordinary artifacts.
Global build/lint/type/format/coverage tooling remains unavailable and is not treated as passing evidence.

changedFileSecurityHandoff.summary: Security handoff should proceed to sdd-review-security. General review confirms the implementation removed active compact-control authority and prepared source-row-first evidence; exhaustive 155-row validation remains owned by review-security.
items: Security-review schema/catalog/template/rules changed under skills/sdd-review-security/ and now declare sourceRowValidation.rows as the only active matrix.
skills/_shared/*, skills/sdd-verify/SKILL.md, skills/sdd-archive/SKILL.md, and agents/sdd/* were propagated to consume canonical JSON summaries/refs only.
python/json_report_to_excel.py defaults sdd-review-security.review-security-report to sourceRowValidation.rows; python/tests and python/README.md align with that default.
Review-security must validate exact-once 155 Source IDs, row evidence, N/A justifications, unsafe evidence rejections, exceptions, and JSON/Markdown parity before verify.

reviewMatrix.standard: Estándar de Diseño y Código WEB
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No portal or UI surface is modified by this change.
The active design simplifies authority: JSON first, derived Markdown, and a single exporter default table path.
No application API or network API is created; CLI behavior is local and covered by tests.
Downstream phases consume summaries/refs and do not duplicate or re-score matrices.
No runtime privilege model changes are introduced; artifact authority boundaries are documented and propagated.
No permission-checking implementation surface is modified.
The small exporter uses clear helper boundaries; no object encapsulation concern is introduced.
No new third-party dependency was added by this change; dependency policy remains openpyxl and pytest only.
Security authority, safe-evidence, exporter, and downstream consumption rules are documented.

reviewMatrix.requirement: activities that can use disproportionate resources handled carefully
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No network or service resource surface is introduced; exporter processes caller-provided local JSON.
Local file operations are bounded; no persistent service resource lifecycle is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: avoid direct buffers for short-lived/infrequently used objects
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No direct buffers or equivalent low-level memory constructs are used.
No service surface is introduced; local CLI error handling is tested.
No numeric resource limits or overflow-sensitive logic are introduced; sheet names are bounded for Excel.
Tests assert secret-like and PII-like payloads do not appear in CLI error output.
No logging of raw report payloads is introduced; evidence remains sanitized.
No sensitive runtime memory handling is added; exporter warning notes unsafe source JSON is not sanitized.

reviewMatrix.evidenceLocation: changed-file inspection summary; tests use placeholder strings only
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No credentials, tokens, connection strings, PAN, PII, or production identifiers were introduced.

reviewMatrix.evidenceLocation: python/tests/test_json_report_to_excel.py workbook read-back tests; this review-report.md read-back
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: Exporter workbook generation is tested through openpyxl read-back; review Markdown is generated from canonical JSON.
No SQL or database access is in scope.

reviewMatrix.requirement: XML and HTML generation handled carefully
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No XML or HTML generation is introduced; Markdown template is derived presentation only.
No XML inclusion behavior is introduced.
Exporter parses JSON as data and does not evaluate code or fetch remote content.
No floating-point validation logic is introduced; scalar values are exported as data.
Derived Markdown and README keep human navigation while preserving JSON authority.
No package visibility or module access-control surface is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: modules used to hide internal packages
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No Java/module system surface is in scope.
No ClassLoader or equivalent runtime loading surface is introduced.
No class hierarchy or override surface is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: superclass impact on subclass behavior understood
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No inheritance-based behavior is introduced.
String handling is limited to sheet-name sanitization and JSON data flattening; validation authority remains upstream.
Exporter accepts local paths from the caller and does not implement privileged filesystem traversal or validation authority.

reviewMatrix.requirement: user input not logged without sanitizing first
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: CLI error tests confirm unsafe source JSON values are not echoed in user-correctable errors.
No archive extraction or ZipInputStream behavior is introduced.
Security validation is delegated to review-security; exporter only selects and flattens already-canonical JSON rows.
No native methods, COM automation, or platform-specific Excel integration are used.
No web form surface is modified.
No mutable value-type API is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: copies created for mutable output values
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No mutable object API is exposed to callers; output is a generated workbook path.

reviewMatrix.requirement: safe copies created for mutable input values and subclasses
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No subclassed mutable input API is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: copy functionality supported for mutable class
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No mutable class copy contract is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: identity equality not trusted when input reference objects can override equality
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No identity/equality trust decision is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: input passed to untrusted object treated as output
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No untrusted object execution boundary is introduced; exporter writes via openpyxl only.

reviewMatrix.requirement: output from untrusted object treated as input
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No untrusted object output processing is introduced beyond JSON data flattening.

reviewMatrix.requirement: wrapper methods around mutable internal state defined
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No mutable internal state is stored by the CLI utility.
Python module constants are not Java public static mutable fields; no applicable surface.

reviewMatrix.requirement: public static final field values ensured constant
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No Java public static final field surface is introduced.
No mutable collection API is exposed as part of this change.

reviewMatrix.requirement: constructors of sensitive classes not exposed
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No sensitive class constructors are introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: private members of outer class not exposed from nested class
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No nested classes are introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: defend against partially initialized instances of non-final classes
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No non-final class construction pattern is introduced.
No constructors or overridable method calls are introduced.
No sensitive class copy behavior is introduced.
JSON serialization is used only to flatten complex cell values; no security-sensitive object serialization is introduced.
Input JSON is parsed as data and top-level shape is validated; security content validation remains upstream.

reviewMatrix.requirement: do not deviate proper serialization method signatures
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No custom serialization method signatures are introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: serialization compatibility enabled during class evolution
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: Report compatibility is handled through schemaVersion and canonical JSON authority, not object serialization compatibility.

reviewMatrix.requirement: instances of inner classes not serialized
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No inner classes are introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: privileges minimized before deserializing from privileged context
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No privileged deserialization context is introduced.
Security report content validation is owned by review-security; exporter requires already safe canonical JSON.
No runtime permission checks are introduced; artifact authority boundary is documented.
No callbacks are introduced.
No Java privileged execution is introduced.
No doPrivileged usage is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: cached results of potentially privileged operations handled carefully
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No privileged-operation result caching is introduced.
No thread/security context transfer is introduced.
No thread construction behavior is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: standard APIs that skip SecurityManager checks based on immediate caller classloader invoked safely
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No caller classloader or SecurityManager API surface is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: standard APIs that use immediate caller classloader invoked safely
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No immediate-caller classloader API surface is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: standard APIs with Java language access checks against immediate caller considered
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No immediate-caller Java access-check surface is introduced.
No Java reflection invocation is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: caller-sensitive method names avoided in interface classes
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No caller-sensitive interface methods are introduced.
No privileged operation results are introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: standard APIs that perform tasks using immediate caller module invoked safely
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No immediate-caller module APIs are introduced.
No InvocationHandler or proxy handler surface is introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: tainted variables not allowed in privileged blocks
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No privileged blocks are introduced; unsafe evidence validation is delegated to review-security.

reviewMatrix.requirement: reflection not used to increase accessibility of classes, methods, or fields
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: No reflection or accessibility escalation is introduced.
No Java/log4j dependency surface is modified or introduced.

reviewMatrix.requirement: security controls not based on untrusted sources
reviewMatrix.observationsComments: Security controls are based on canonical source-row JSON and catalog refs; compact-control authority is removed from active contracts.
